trial_identity.brief_title: Phase 2 Placebo-Controlled Study to Assess the Safety and Efficacy of ESK-001 in Active Systemic Lupus Erythematosus
trial_identity.official_title: A Phase 2, Multicenter, Multinational, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-Controlled Study to Assess the Safety, Efficacy, and Pharmacokinetics of Multiple Dose Levels of ESK-001 in Adult Patients With Systemic Lupus Erythematosus

missing_data_flags.reason: Asset identity requires clinical review because deterministic resolution has ambiguity signals.

primary_endpoint_measures: To compare the effect on disease activity measured by the proportion of patients achieving British Isles Lupus Assessment Group (BILAG)-based Composite Lupus Assessment (BICLA) response at Week 48 between doses of ESK-001 and placebo
secondary_endpoint_measures: To assess the safety and tolerability of multiple dose levels of ESK-001
To compare the effect on disease activity measured by the proportion of patients achieving an SLE Responder Index of ≥4 (SRI[4]) response at Week 48
To compare corticosteroid use in patients at Week 48

To compare the effect on cutaneous disease activity measured by the proportion of patients with a CLASI activity score of ≥8 at baseline achieving ≥ 50% reduction in the CLASI activity score at Week 48 between doses of ESK-001 and placebo
To compare the effect on disease activity measured by the proportion of patients achieving BICLA responses at Week 48 between doses of ESK-001 and placebo by stratification groups
To compare the Lupus Low Disease Activity State (LLDAS) response between doses of ESK-001 and placebo at Week 48
To compare the annualized flare rate through Week 48
To use the SF-36 (Short Form-36 item QoL measure) to compare the effect on health-related quality of life (HRQOL) between doses of ESK-001 and placebo
To use the SLE-specific Lupus Quality of Life (LQoL) questionnaire to compare the effect on health-related quality of life (HRQOL) between doses of ESK-001 and placebo
To compare disease-specific QoL between doses of ESK-001 and placebo
To compare disease-specific QoL between doses of ESK-001 and placebo
To compare disease-specific QoL between doses of ESK-001 and placebo
To compare Fatigue measured by FACIT-F between doses of ESK-001 and placebo
To compare patient global assessment of disease activity (PtGA) between doses of ESK-001 and placebo
trial_design_features.eligibility_summary: Inclusion Criteria: Patients with 6 or more months of SLE according to the 2019 EULAR/ACR criteria, have positive autoantibodies or low complement at screening, and have active SLE as measured by SLEDAI-2K of 6 or more, or 4 or more if joint involvement is present. Patients need to be on treatment which can be: * A stable dose of oral corticosteroid (≤40 mg/day prednisone or equivalent) for a minimum of 2 weeks prior to signing of the informed consent form (ICF) at the Screening Visit. The dose

comparator_benchmarking.benchmark_summary: ClinicalTrials.gov search found 3 public comparator trials for SLE and PHASE2. Comparator relevance review classified 0 relevant, 1 weak, and 2 excluded CT.gov candidates.
comparator_benchmarking.sponsor_summary: Buddhist Tzu Chi General Hospital: 1; Merck Healthcare KGaA, Darmstadt, Germany, an affiliate of Merck KGaA, Darmstadt, Germany: 1; Miltenyi Biomedicine GmbH: 1
comparator_benchmarking.endpoint_summary: 5 primary endpoints were normalized across 3 trials.

flags.reason: Trial protocol data were retrieved from the public ClinicalTrials.gov API.
Asset identity used RxNorm when matched and shared deterministic rules/config.
Historical PoS lookup used the local workbook when a phase and disease-area row matched.
Public label safety context is included only when openFDA returns a usable label match.
Bounded PubMed evidence extraction is outside this pass; no literature claims were generated.
AACT was optional and not added in this pass.
CTOD integration is explicitly out of scope for this pass.
External trial-outcome models are explicitly out of scope for this pass.

flags.reason: Commercial databases and proprietary RWE sources are explicitly out of scope.

claims.claim_text: NCT05966480 has ClinicalTrials.gov status ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING and phases PHASE2.
NCT05966480 reports enrollment 408 and 1 primary endpoints.
Endpoint risk level is low based on registry primary endpoint structure.
Enrollment and duration risk level is low.
ClinicalTrials.gov comparator benchmarking matched 3 public trials.
Failure-mode classification overall risk level is medium.
The inferred clinical asset for NCT05966480 is Envudeucitinib.
Historical PoS estimate is 0.193 for Autoimmune Phase II.
Approval likelihood proxy is 0.193 using the source-backed historical PoS basis.
Clinical label-expansion rationale was structured from registry condition context and available public label context.
